title: Type-State Pattern State Machine
description: Implement a compile-time enforced state machine using Rust's type-state pattern, ensuring invalid state transitions are caught at compile time.
requirements: Model a TCP connection: Closed -> Listen -> Established -> Closed
Transition methods consume self and return new state
Invalid transitions should fail at compile time
Implement data that persists across state transitions
Use PhantomData if needed for state markers
Implement methods only available in specific states
Support state-specific behavior (e.g., send only when Established)
